# EV Mapping

> Free, open-source electric vehicle range calculator and charging station finder.
> URL: https://ev-mapping.vercel.app/

## What it does

EV Mapping answers two core questions for electric vehicle drivers and shoppers:

1. **How far can I go?** — One-way range and round-trip radius based on your EV model, starting location, and current charge level.
2. **Where can I charge?** — All charging stations within your range shown on a live map, with address and a Google Maps navigation link.

## How to use it

1. Select your EV model from 700+ presets (2025–2026 models, 20+ markets)
2. Enter your starting location (city, address, or landmark)
3. Set your current charge level (1–100%) using the slider
4. Press **Compute Reach** to instantly see:
   - One-way range in km
   - Round-trip radius in km
   - Number of charging stations within reach
   - Interactive map with range circle and charger pins

## Common questions this tool answers

- "How far can I drive my [EV model] on a full charge?"
- "How far can I go on 80% battery?"
- "Can my electric car make it from [City A] to [City B] and back without charging?"
- "Where are EV charging stations near me?"
- "What is the real-world range of a 2025 Tesla Model 3 / BMW iX / Hyundai Ioniq 6 / Kia EV6?"
- "Which EV has the longest range?"
- "How many charging stations are within driving distance of [location]?"

## Supported vehicles

700+ presets covering major brands: Tesla, BMW, Audi, Hyundai, Kia, BYD, Volkswagen, Rivian, Volvo, Mercedes-Benz, Polestar, Porsche, Ford, GM/Chevrolet, Nissan, Renault, Stellantis, Xpeng, NIO, and more.

Model years: 2025–2026. Auto-updated every 12 hours via GitHub Actions.

## Supported markets (20+)

United States, United Kingdom, Germany, France, India, Australia, Canada, Singapore, China, Netherlands, Norway, Sweden, Italy, Spain, Belgium, Austria, Switzerland, New Zealand, South Korea, Japan.

## Data sources

| Data | Source |
|------|--------|
| EV specs & prices | Auto-synced catalog (manufacturer data, updated every 12h) |
| Charging stations | OpenChargeMap API + OpenStreetMap Overpass API |
| Geocoding | Nominatim (OpenStreetMap) |
| Currency conversion | Frankfurter API |

## Features

- **Range calculation** — accounts for battery capacity, real-world efficiency, and battery reserve
- **Charge level slider** — set any charge from 1–100%, not just full
- **Charging station map** — live pins from two independent open-data sources, merged and de-duplicated
- **Google Maps link** — one tap to navigate to any charging station
- **Compare EVs** — side-by-side range and spec comparison for up to 3 models
- **URL sharing** — shareable links preserve all settings
- **GPS support** — use browser geolocation as starting point
- **PWA** — installable, offline-capable via service worker
- **No login, no account, completely free**

## URL Parameters (for sharing and deep-linking)

- `car` — EV preset ID (e.g. `tesla-model-3-rwd`)
- `loc` — Start location string (e.g. `San Francisco, CA`)
- `soc` — State of charge % (default: `100`)
- `batt` — Battery kWh (custom mode only)
- `eff` — Consumption kWh/100km (custom mode only)
- `res` — Battery reserve % (default: `10`)
- `src` — Data source: `auto` | `openchargemap` | `overpass` (default: `auto`)
- `vp` — Verification profile: `independent` | `official` (default: `independent`)

## Limitations

- Range is an estimate; real-world range varies with speed, terrain, weather, and driving style
- Charger data from public APIs — coverage varies by region and may lag real-world availability
- Public APIs may rate-limit; the app falls back gracefully and still shows range circles

## Technical

- Vanilla JS (ES modules), Leaflet.js map, hosted on Vercel (static)
- Open source: https://github.com/novelmartis/ev-mapping
- License: MIT
